original_hypothesis.title: PairFactory setReferralFee lacks upper bound allowing DoS of all swaps
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The setReferralFee function does not enforce a maximum limit. If set above 10000, it causes an arithmetic underflow in the Pair contract's fee calculation, permanently DoSing all swaps.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In PairFactory, setReferralFee allows the feeManager to set MAX_REFERRAL_FEE to any value. In Pair._update0, _referralFee is calculated as amount * MAX_REFERRAL_FEE / 10000. If MAX_REFERRAL_FEE > 10000, _referralFee will be greater than amount. The subsequent line amount -= _referralFee will underflow and revert. This halts all trading across all pairs in the protocol.
preconditions: feeManager role is compromised or acts maliciously/erroneously

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin sets fee > 100% to halt the protocol
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker holding short positions elsewhere could profit from protocol halt

economic_questions: Is the feeManager a multisig or a single EOA?
Can this be used as a griefing attack by a rogue team member?
validation_requirements: Verify all swaps revert due to underflow

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete halt of protocol trading operations

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Inability to trade or manage positions effectively

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
